blocks.content: The field was bigger than I expected. Sixty kids maybe, all of them looking like they'd been here before.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye — stay close to me until Coach calls the groups. After that you're on your own.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I'm serious. Out here it's just you and the clock. Nobody can help you run.

Coach Briggs came out of the field house last. He was built like someone who never stopped training — not heavy, disciplined. Rugged in the face, like the years hadn't softened anything. The field went quiet when he walked onto it. Not because he said anything. Just because of how he moved.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: I'm not here to see who wants it. Everybody out here wants it. I'm here to see who has it when it gets hard. There's a difference.

blocks.speaker: Coach Briggs
blocks.content: We go in groups. Skill positions first. Then line. Run the routes I call. Do not run the routes you think I want to see — run the ones I call. Any questions?
Jonathan looked at me once. Not for reassurance — just acknowledgment. Then he walked to the line like he'd been waiting two years for this moment. Which he had.
